steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a noun ('formation') followed by a dependent clause ('some biologists believe can be used to explain...'). The question tests how to correctly attach that clause to the noun.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — a comma before 'some': A comma separates the noun from its modifying clause, breaking the connection. ✗
  • Choice B — a semicolon before 'some': A semicolon signals an independent clause, but what follows is dependent. ✗
  • Choice C — 'formation that some': 'That' is the correct relative pronoun to introduce a clause modifying a noun, and no punctuation before it is needed. ✓
  • Choice D — 'formation, that some': A comma before 'that' is incorrect when introducing a restrictive relative clause. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
